title: IAEA chief says Iran's nuclear sites will be inspected, Tehran bars access until final deal
cleaned_news: The head of the United Nations’ nuclear watchdog said Wednesday that inspectors will eventually gain access to Iran’s uranium enrichment facilities, a critical component of the interim agreement reached between the United States and Iran, but Iran quickly challenged this assertion, stating that any discussion of inspections at enrichment facilities would only occur as part of a final, comprehensive accord, underscoring ongoing tensions surrounding implementation of the agreement.
bullets: • The head of the United Nations’ nuclear watchdog, IAEA Director General Rafael Mariano Grossi, expressed confidence that inspections would proceed.
• Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi said any discussion of inspections at enrichment facilities would only occur as part of a final, comprehensive accord.
• The dispute centers on Iran’s enrichment facilities, which have remained inaccessible to IAEA inspectors since the 12-day war between Israel and Iran in 2025.
• A central provision of the interim US-Iran deal requires Tehran to reduce the enrichment level of its uranium stockpile through a process known as downblending.
• In exchange, Washington has agreed to ease sanctions targeting Iran’s oil sector while both sides work toward a broader agreement within a 60-day negotiating window.

vocabularies.meaning_en: Temporary or provisional; in the meantime.

error_spotting.sentence: The head of the United Nations’ nuclear watchdog say Wednesday that inspectors will eventually gain access to Iran’s uranium enrichment facilities.
error_spotting.rule: The past tense form of the verb 'say' is 'said'. In reported speech or when referring to a past action, the verb must agree with the past tense context.

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: The arrangement is widely viewed as a _____ confidence-building measure intended to prevent renewed escalation following the 2025 conflict.

jumbled_parts: that the nuclear activities
The accord says explicitly
with regards to the nuclear material facilities
will be supervised by the IAEA

title: Airfares may climb up to 25% with doubling of fuel costs in 2026: Report
cleaned_news: Geopolitical disruption and refinery constraints are impacting jet fuel supply, which raises airline costs and could translate into higher crack spreads, thereby pushing airfares, according to a McKinsey report, noting jet fuel demand is set to rise ahead of the summer travel season amid depleted inventories, and fuel prices have surged mainly due to crude oil trends while supply has been constrained, leading to a potential doubling of fuel costs that could lead to fare increases of roughly 20 to 25 percent.
bullets: • Geopolitical disruption and refinery constraints are impacting jet fuel supply, raising airline costs.
• Jet fuel demand is set to rise ahead of the summer travel season amid depleted inventories.
• Fuel prices have surged mainly due to crude oil trends, while supply has been constrained by reduced refinery production.
• The jet fuel "crack spread" measures the difference in price between crude oil and refined fuel products.
• A doubling of fuel costs could lead to fare increases of roughly 20 to 25 percent, as about 30 percent of an airline ticket price goes toward fuel.

vocabularies.meaning_en: restricted or limited in scope or freedom.

error_spotting.sentence: Geopolitical disruption and refinery constraints is impacting jet fuel supply.
error_spotting.rule: Subject-verb agreement: When a sentence has a compound subject joined by 'and', the verb should be plural.

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: Jet fuel demand is set to rise ahead of the summer travel season amid depleted _____.

jumbled_parts: A: Geopolitical disruption and refinery constraints
B: thereby pushing airfares, according to a McKinsey report.
C: are impacting jet fuel supply, which raises airline costs
D: and could translate into higher crack spreads,

title: Piyush Goyal to visit UK from 25-27 June to finalize trade deal
cleaned_news: Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal will visit the United Kingdom from 25-27 June to review preparations for the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) and the Double Contribution Convention (DCC) and to further strengthen economic ties. During his visit, Goyal will hold bilateral meetings, participate in government-to-business engagements, and address the India Global Forum, focusing on opportunities arising from CETA. The discussions will align regulatory roadmaps, streamline customs coordination, and finalize administrative mechanisms for CETA's tariff liberalization and DCC's operational roadmap, supporting the objective of expanding bilateral trade to $120 billion by 2030.
bullets: • Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal will visit the United Kingdom from 25-27 June.
• The visit precedes the coming into force of the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) and the Double Contribution Convention (DCC) on 15 July.
• Goyal will hold a bilateral meeting with Peter Kyle, the UK’s secretary of state for business and trade, to review preparations for the two agreements.
• Discussions will focus on aligning regulatory roadmaps, streamlining cross-border customs coordination, and finalising administrative mechanisms for CETA and DCC.
• The visit supports the shared objective of expanding bilateral trade between India and the UK to $120 billion by 2030.

vocabularies.meaning_en: making a process or system more efficient by simplifying it.
a pledge or undertaking to do something.

error_spotting.sentence: Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal will visit the United Kingdom from 25-27 June, before the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) and the Double Contribution Convention (DCC) comes into force on 15 July.
error_spotting.rule: Subject-verb agreement: When two or more subjects are joined by 'and', the verb should be plural. Here, 'CETA and DCC' is a plural subject, requiring the plural verb 'come'.

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: The ministry said the visit underscores India's commitment to engaging with global businesses and investors and supports the shared _____ of expanding bilateral trade between India and the UK to $120 billion by 2030.

jumbled_parts: A: During his visit, Goyal will hold a bilateral meeting
B: with Peter Kyle, the UK’s secretary of state for business and trade,
C: to review preparations for the two agreements
D: and to further strengthen economic ties between India and the UK.

title: RBI says no to special exposure relaxations for state NBFCs
cleaned_news: The RBI said that while a case-by-case exemption was permitted earlier, most of such exemptions had a sunset clause which has since expired, and the exposure of these entities have been brought within limits. The RBI has, however, allowed any residual breaches under the revised concentration norms to be grandfathered, and such exposures can be offset by using eligible credit risk transfer instruments such as state government guarantees. The RBI has allowed government-owned NBFCs to breach their single and group exposure limits provided these are run off until maturity and that net incremental exposure remains unchanged. In amended directions, the RBI also increased the large exposure limit for infrastructure finance companies (IFCs) in the upper layer to 45% from 35% of the capital base.
bullets: • Earlier, case-by-case exemptions were permitted for state NBFCs.
• Most of these exemptions had a sunset clause which has since expired.
• The exposure of these entities has been brought within limits by the RBI.
• Any residual breaches under the revised concentration norms are allowed to be grandfathered.
• The RBI increased the large exposure limit for infrastructure finance companies (IFCs) in the upper layer to 45% from 35% of the capital base.

vocabularies.meaning_en: came to an end; no longer valid

error_spotting.sentence: The exposure of these entities have been brought within limits.
error_spotting.rule: The subject 'exposure' is singular, so the singular verb 'has' should be used instead of the plural 'have' to maintain subject-verb agreement.

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: The RBI has, however, allowed any residual breaches under the revised concentration norms to be _____.

jumbled_parts: The RBI has allowed government-owned NBFCs
to breach their single and group exposure limits
provided these are run off until maturity
and that net incremental exposure remains unchanged.

title: RBI in wait-and-watch mode, no certainty on rate hike: Governor Sanjay Malhotra
cleaned_news: Malhotra said that if it was so certain that we are going to hike rates in the coming months, then we would have changed the stance from neutral to restrictive, but we did not do that precisely because there is elevated uncertainty. He cautioned that the global environment and the progress of monsoon remain uncertain, and assured that the RBI is keeping an eye on these factors, being in a wait-and-watch mode. The RBI will keep an eye on both crude oil prices and the progress of monsoon to take a view on inflation, as there could be risks on both inflation and growth. In the monetary policy committee (MPC) meeting on June 5, the RBI unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, while acknowledging that the risks of higher inflation had amplified, and decided to maintain a neutral policy stance. The RBI has revised its consumer price inflation forecast upwards citing higher global crude oil prices and their knock-on effects on fuel, industrial inputs and raw materials, and also revised growth forecast for this fiscal downwards.
bullets: • Governor Malhotra stated that the RBI is in a wait-and-watch mode due to elevated uncertainty regarding the global environment and monsoon progress.
• The RBI did not change its policy stance from neutral to restrictive, indicating no certainty on a rate hike in the coming months.
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% in its June 5 meeting.
• The RBI revised its consumer price inflation forecast upwards, expecting it to increase to 5.1% in 2026-27 from 4.6% forecast earlier.
• India's retail inflation accelerated to 3.93% year-on-year in May from 3.48% in April, remaining below the RBI's medium-term target of 4%.

vocabularies.meaning_en: raised or increased to a higher level

error_spotting.sentence: The RBI will keep an eye on both crude oil prices and the progress of monsoon to takes a view on inflation.
error_spotting.rule: The infinitive form of a verb (base form) must follow 'to' when expressing purpose or intention.

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: We are in a wait-and-watch mode because the monsoon has been weak so far but, just to _____, we have enough food stocks.

jumbled_parts: The RBI has revised its consumer price inflation forecast upwards
citing higher global crude oil prices
and their knock-on effects on fuel,
industrial inputs and raw materials.

title: S&P cuts India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.6%, sees inflation at 5.1%
cleaned_news: S&P cuts India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.6%, sees inflation at 5.1%. The US-headquartered ratings agency expects gross domestic product (GDP) growth to recover to 7.2% in 2027-28. Retail inflation in India is forecast to rise to 5.1% in 2026-27 as manufacturers pass on higher energy costs to consumers, alongside recent increases in administered prices. S&P's baseline forecast assumes that disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz will gradually ease, but a prolonged energy disruption would be unfavourable for the Asia-Pacific region, pushing inflation up and weighing on economic growth.
bullets: • S&P cuts India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.6%.
• S&P forecasts India's retail inflation to rise to 5.1% in 2026-27.
• The US-headquartered ratings agency expects India's GDP growth to recover to 7.2% in 2027-28.
• S&P's baseline forecast assumes disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz will gradually ease.
• The ratings agency expects a policy rate hike to 5.5% in the second half of this fiscal.

vocabularies.meaning_en: raised to a higher position or level

error_spotting.sentence: The US-headquartered ratings agency expect gross domestic product (GDP) growth to recover to 7.2% in 2027-28.
error_spotting.rule: Subject-verb agreement: A singular subject ('agency') requires a singular verb ('expects').

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: Retail inflation in India is forecast to rise to 5.1% in 2026-27 as manufacturers pass on higher energy costs to consumers, alongside recent increases in _____ prices such as those for petrol, diesel and cooking gas.

jumbled_parts: Higher energy costs would increase the prices
of goods and services, pushing inflation
in China, India and Japan up 0.5-0.6 percentage points
in the third quarter of 2026.
